claim_text: No single candidate dominates all mandates and latency tiers simultaneously
caveat: Binary vs vol-target tradeoff is fundamental

claim_text: LATCH is a near-duplicate of SM with no measurable structural or operational differentiation
LATCH replay fragility is identical to SM across all 3 disruption classes
EMA(21d) overlay increases baseline Sharpe (+0.060 harsh) while adding delay fragility (+0.036 worse D4 delta)
caveat: E0_plus still dominates on absolute Sharpe at all tested delay levels

claim_text: EMA(21d) overlay increases E5 baseline Sharpe (+0.073 harsh) but creates performance crossover at D3-D4
caveat: E5 overtakes E5_plus beyond D2-D3 delay

claim_text: E5_plus_EMA1D21 is the highest-performing candidate under LT1 (Sharpe 1.430 harsh)
caveat: WFO 5/8 is weaker than E0_plus 6/8; fragile beyond LT1

claim_text: E0_plus_EMA1D21 is the only strategy to pass ALL Tier 1 validation gates including WFO 6/8
caveat: bootstrap Sharpe_med=0.562 is lowest of binary class

claim_text: E0 is the least delay-fragile binary candidate (D4 delta = -0.336 vs -0.372 to -0.517 for peers)
caveat: Still loses 29.5% of Sharpe at D4 — not operationally robust

claim_text: SM is the only candidate viable under LT3 / manual execution (4% Sharpe loss at D4 vs 29-41% for binary)
caveat: CAGR 16.0% is the price of operational robustness

claim_text: No binary candidate is acceptable for manual or semi-manual deployment (all lose >29% Sharpe at D4)
All 6 candidates are home-run dependent in unit-size view (zero-cross within 6-14 trades)
caveat: SM/LATCH are hybrid in native view due to vol-target sizing

claim_text: Random missed entries are nearly harmless (CV 0.32-1.51% at K=1; 100% of draws Sharpe-positive)
Entry delay is the dominant operational fragility axis (not random miss or outage)
supporting_metric_value: binary: 29-41% loss at D4 vs outage: 5.6%

claim_text: Step 5 is not needed for research conclusion — only for live sign-off hardening
caveat: Step 5 could refine confidence but not change ranking

claim_text: Binary candidates show perfect inverse correlation between baseline Sharpe and delay robustness
Single-week outages cause moderate bounded degradation (5.6-9.4% Sharpe loss worst-case)
Skipping after 2 consecutive losses is universally harmful (delta Sharpe -0.159 to -0.263)
